title: Magneto-mechanical marker with enhanced frequency stability and signal strength
abstract: A magneto mechanical resonator device comprises a ferromagnetic resonator element having a length substantially equal to an integer multiple (N) of a Â½ wavelength of a resonant frequency of the ferromagnetic resonator element, wherein the ferromagnetic resonator element is formed from an amorphous metal material, wherein N is at least 2, and at least one bias magnetic element disposed along a lateral side of the ferromagnetic resonator element.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A magneto mechanical resonator device, comprising: a ferromagnetic resonator element having a length substantially equal to an integer multiple (N) of a Â½ wavelength of a resonant frequency of the ferromagnetic resonator element, the ferromagnetic resonator element formed from an amorphous metal material, and wherein N is at least 2; and at least one bias magnetic element disposed along a lateral side of the ferromagnetic resonator element. 2. A magneto mechanical resonator device of claim 1, further comprising a plurality of ferromagnetic elements disposed in a stacked arrangement. 3. The magneto mechanical resonator device of claim 1, further comprising a housing to enclose the resonator device. 4. The magneto mechanical resonator device of claim 1, wherein each of the ferromagnetic elements comprises a ferromagnetic metallic strip having a length of about 78 - 84 mm. 5. The magneto mechanical resonator device of claim 1, further comprising a plurality of ferromagnetic elements having a stacked arrangement. 6. The magneto mechanical resonator device of claim 5, further comprising a substrate to support the stacked arrangement. 7. The magneto mechanical resonator device of claim 1, further comprising a housing to enclose the ferromagnetic resonator element and at least on bias magnetic element. 8. The magneto mechanical resonator device of claim 1, wherein the resonator device has a frequency range of from about 34 kHz to about 80 kHz. 9. The magneto mechanical resonator device of claim 1, wherein the resonator device can be detected at a depth up to about 60 inches underground. 10. The magneto mechanical resonator device of claim 7, wherein the housing has sufficient flexibility to be mounted to a coiled pipe. 11. The magneto mechanical resonator device of claim 7, wherein the housing comprises a substrate to support the ferromagnetic resonator element and cover to enclose the ferromagnetic resonator element and the at least one bias magnet. 12. The magneto mechanical resonator device of claim 11, wherein at least one of the substrate and housing comprises one or more slots located along each side of the ferromagnetic resonator element that are positioned at end and/or central locations along the length of the ferromagnetic resonator element. 13. The magneto mechanical resonator device of claim 1, comprising a first bias magnetic element disposed at a first side edge location of the ferromagnetic resonator element and a second bias magnetic element disposed a second side edge location of the ferromagnetic resonator element, wherein the first side location is opposite the second side location. 14. The magneto mechanical resonator device of claim 13, wherein the first bias magnetic element is disposed at the first side edge location of the ferromagnetic resonator element at a position equidistant from first and second end portions of the ferromagnetic resonator element and the second bias magnetic element is disposed at the second side edge location of the ferromagnetic resonator element at a position equidistant from first and second end portions of the ferromagnetic resonator element, wherein the first and second bias magnetic elements have the same respective magnetic poles facing each other such that each Â½ wavelength segment is biased in an opposite longitudinal direction. 15. The magneto mechanical resonator device of claim 1, wherein the at least one bias magnetic element comprises a single bias magnetic element disposed along a lateral side edge at a position equidistant from first and second end portions of the 

 ferromagnetic resonator element, wherein each Â½ wavelength segment is biased in an opposite longitudinal direction. 16. The magneto mechanical resonator device of claim 1, comprising: first and second bias magnetic elements disposed opposite one another along opposing lateral side edges of the ferromagnetic resonator element at a first end portion of the ferromagnetic resonator element; and third and fourth bias magnetic elements disposed opposite one another along the opposing lateral side edges of the ferromagnetic resonator element at a second end portion of the ferromagnetic resonator element, wherein the first, second, third, and fourth bias magnetic elements have the same magnetic poles facing the ferromagnetic resonator element such that each Â½ wavelength segment is biased in an opposite longitudinal direction. 17. The magneto mechanical resonator device of claim 1, comprising: first and second bias magnetic elements disposed opposite one another along opposing side edges at a first end portion of the ferromagnetic resonator element; third and fourth bias magnetic elements disposed opposite one another along opposing side edges at a central longitudinal position along the length of the ferromagnetic resonator element being located equidistant from the first end portion and a second end portion of the ferromagnetic resonator element; fifth and sixth bias magnetic elements disposed opposite one another along opposing side edges at the second end portion of the ferromagnetic resonator element, wherein the first, second, fifth and sixth bias magnetic elements have the same first magnetic pole facing the ferromagnetic resonator element and the third and fourth bias magnetic elements the same second magnetic pole facing the ferromagnetic resonator element such that each Â½ wavelength segment is biased in an opposite longitudinal direction. 18. The magneto mechanical resonator device of claim 11, wherein the first side location corresponds to a beginning of a Â½ wavelength segment of the ferromagnetic resonator element. 19. The magneto mechanical resonator device of claim 1, wherein a length of the ferromagnetic resonator element is substantially equal to at least 2 full wavelengths of a resonant frequency of the ferromagnetic resonator element. 20. The magneto mechanical resonator device of claim 1, wherein N equals an even number of 2 or more. 21. A magneto mechanical marker system comprising the magneto mechanical resonator device of claim 1 and a portable locating device. 22. A magneto mechanical resonator device comprising an amorphous metal ribbon having a length that is an integer multiple (N) of a 1/2 wavelength of a corresponding resonant frequency, the ribbon being subjected to a differential alternating biasing. 23. The magneto mechanical resonator device of claim 22, wherein the differential alternating biasing comprises at least one bias magnetic element disposed along a lateral side of the ribbon. 24. The magneto mechanical resonator device of claim 22, wherein the ribbon includes a plurality of Â½ wavelength segments, wherein each adjacent 1/2 wavelength segment is biased in an opposite direction. 25. A magneto mechanical resonator device comprising an amorphous metal ribbon, wherein the ribbon includes a plurality of Â½ wavelength segments, wherein each 1/2 wavelength segment has a length corresponding to a length of a Â½ wavelength of the resonant frequency of the ribbon, and wherein each adjacent 1/2 wavelength segment is biased in an opposite direction.
